claim: Evidence schema, prompt, readiness contract, and tests all distinguish project, external, user, and unknown evidence scopes.

evidence.summary: Schema defines VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES constant and implements scope validation/classification functions; readiness contract enforces scope declaration with blockers
evidence.why: Router.ts implements the core scope distinction logic in schema definition, validation, and PRD readiness enforcement.

evidence.summary: Runtime scope validation blocks invalid results; agent prompt instructs evidence agents to declare scopes correctly
evidence.why: Evidence-dispatch.ts provides both the agent instructions (prompt) and runtime validation that enforce scope distinction.

evidence.summary: Tests verify external evidence is rejected for project facts, project evidence requires file locators, scope declaration is mandatory, and cross-check requires all three evidence agents
evidence.why: Test suite provides comprehensive coverage of scope distinction enforcement across PRD readiness contract and validation logic.

evidence.summary: Test verifies runtime scope validation blocks agent results without explicit scope declaration
evidence.why: Test confirms evidence-dispatch runtime enforcement rejects results missing scope field.

evidence.summary: Doctrine documents evidence scope requirements and three-agent protocol (explorer, cross-checker, verifier)
evidence.why: Documentation explains the design intent and formal contract for evidence scope distinction.

risks: If scope distinction enforcement is incomplete or incorrect, existing-project facts could be accepted based on external research or user assertions, leading to incorrect PRDs that do not match the actual codebase

result.notes: Verifier confirms: (1) No assumptions promoted to facts; both explorer and cross-checker have empty assumptions arrays and all 16 evidence records are backed by concrete file paths with line numbers. (2) Evidence paths are usable; all cited files and line ranges are specific and verifiable. (3) No unresolved missing data; both agents report missing=[] and assumptions=[]. (4) Full agent agreement; explorer and cross-checker independently reached identical conclusions with high confidence. All four components (schema, prompt, readiness contract, tests) explicitly distinguish evidence scopes with validation and blockers. Cross-check PRD readiness can proceed.
